# **CHAPTER 01: IDEATION** {#chapter-01:-ideation}

## **1.1 Problem statement** {#1.1-problem-statement}

Most individuals in the current age undergo various physical and mental health problems due to their sedentary lifestyles, academic stress, and lack of sustainable motivation in traditional fitness apps. Even with upcoming platforms which have allowed users to track their physical fitness and improve their mental health wellbeing and conduct activities even such as nutrition tracking, most of these applications fail in the stage of keeping their customer base consistently tracking their habits. This can be directly attributed to the boring interfaces and traditional ways that try to essentially force users to build up their health habits. The root cause for the 3 week retention problem essentially stems from users simply losing interest in these repetitive and boring applications. This is what we aim to solve in our business proposal.

## **1.2 Idea** {#1.2-idea}

VitalQuest is a gamified digital platform designed to improve fitness, physical health, and psychological well-being through an immersive role-playing game (RPG) experience. The platform allows users to progress a personalized digital avatar by completing real-world health activities such as exercise, nutrition tracking, and mindfulness practices.

By synchronizing data from wearable devices, VitalQuest automatically verifies user activity and converts it into in-game rewards, experience points, and narrative progression. The system promotes balanced well-being by linking physical exertion and mental energy, ensuring users develop sustainable habits rather than extreme routines. Through storytelling, quests, and progression systems, VitalQuest transforms everyday health activities into engaging and meaningful experiences.

## **1.3 Revenue generation** {#1.3-revenue-generation}

VitalQuest follows a freemium revenue model to attract a large user base while encouraging premium upgrades.

Revenue is generated through:

* Subscription plans that unlock advanced quests, detailed analytics, narrative expansions, and expert-guided fitness and mental wellness modules  
* In-app purchases, including avatar customizations, special challenges, and premium storylines  
* Corporate wellness packages offered to organizations seeking to improve employee well-being and productivity  
* Partnerships and sponsorships with fitness, wellness, and wearable technology brands

This model ensures recurring revenue while maintaining accessibility for free users.

## **1.4 Target market** {#1.4-target-market}

The primary target market for VitalQuest consists of digitally active individuals aged 18–45 who are interested in improving their physical and mental well-being.

Primary audience:

* University students managing academic stress  
* Working professionals with sedentary lifestyles  
* Fitness beginners seeking motivation and structure

Secondary audience:

* Fitness trainers and wellness coaches using digital tools  
* Corporate organizations implementing employee wellness programs

The platform is designed to support users of varying fitness levels, making it inclusive and adaptable to individual goals and capabilities.

## **1.5 Mission statement** {#1.5-mission-statement}

VitalQuest’s mission is to empower individuals to achieve balanced physical and psychological well-being by transforming healthy habits into an engaging, rewarding, and sustainable digital experience.

## **1.6 Rich picture diagram** {#1.6-rich-picture-diagram}

![][image1]

## **1.7 Categorizations and business model** {#1.7-categorizations-and-business-model}

**1.7.1 Categorizations**

VitalQuest is a dotcom company that has no physical locations and only operates via its mobile app and online platform. This is consistent with the dotcom model, which depends on internet-based infrastructure for incentive distribution, wearable data synchronization, and user involvement (Yadav, n.d). Every VitalQuest activity takes place in a completely virtual environment, from activity verification and quest advancement to analytics and premium unlocks. This characterizes it as a pure e-business, with all operations carried out using digital networks and APIs. VitalQuest is dominated by the Business-to-Consumer (B2C) model, which offers individual consumers customized avatar advancement and habit-building tools for long-term wellbeing. Additionally, it includes a Business-to-Business (B2B) component through brand collaborations and corporate wellness packages, giving companies access to sponsored challenges and group dashboards (Scope of e-Business : B2B | B2C | C2C | Intra B-Commerce, 2025).

**1.7.2 E-business model**

* **Freemium Model:** To attract a larger user base, especially in the initial stages, VitalQuest provides basic services without any charges. Advanced narrative-driven quests and analytics can be unlocked with paid versions (Freemium: Definition, Best Practices, Benefits and Examples, n.d).  
* Similar to successful gamified apps, this generates recurrent revenue and user advancement.  
* **In-App Purchases and Affiliate Model:** In-app purchases are made available where users can customize their avatars. This increases ecosystem value and diversifies revenue.

## **1.8 Competitor analysis** {#1.8-competitor-analysis}

### **1.8.1 Competitors** {#1.8.1-competitors}

* **Habitica \-** A leading global productivity platform aiding people in building new habits and keeping them motivated to achieve set personal goals. This platform makes use of 8-bit RPG mechanics to achieve this functionality and treats daily tasks as quests where users can earn gold and equipment for their avatars.

* **Zombies, Run\! \-** An eccentric fitness app using immersive storytelling to turn running and walking in the real-world into survival missions in a zombie apocalypse. A pioneering platform in narrative driven fitness.

* **Strava \-** A social network platform emphasizing on competitive running and cycling. Uses GPS data to create leaderboards and personalized routes to carry out the users preferred physical activities.

* **SuperBetter \-** A gamified app with clinical support that promotes mental health and resilience. "Power-ups" and "quests" are used to help people cope with stress and heal from injuries or illnesses and promote physical and mental wellbeing.

* **MyFitnessPal \-**  A well-known utilitarian health platform that emphasizes tracking calories and nutrients. It interacts with most major wearables and has one of the biggest food databases in the world. 

### **1.8.2 Comparative analysis** {#1.8.2-comparative-analysis}

| Company | Strengths | Weaknesses |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Habitica** | Social guild features with active player base for accountability; strong RPG nostalgia hook | Easy to cheat on set goals and daily tasks as it relies on manual data entry |
| **Zombies, Run\!** | Immersive narration building a strong connection between the users and platform; works offline | Available activities are restricted to cardio activities |
| **Strava** | Popular among serious athletes, has robust social features allowing competition among users with high accuracy GPS tracking | Can project intimidation onto beginners and lacks mental health support and restricted physical activity |
| **SuperBetter** | Backed by strong scientific/physiological evidence; focusing on resilience and mental recovery | Dated interface compared to cutting edge RPG applications and lacks modern graphics |
| **MyFitnessPal** | A massive food database and a clinical utility popular among a large user base | Severely lacks gamification and employs a chore like interface leading to users losing consistency over time |
| **VitalQuest** | Verified progress in individuals where users enter a RPG and make progress through their digital avatars following progressive  storylines | Lack of a large user base initially could potentially hinder the community driven features in the application  |

### **1.8.3 Competitive advantage** {#1.8.3-competitive-advantage}

* **Verified progress:**  Unlike other applications which rely on users manually entering the progress of their tasks, our application uses a proprietary engine “Bio-Sync” ensuring seamless connectivity. Achieving this functionality is made possible by pulling verified information from wearables like heart rate, sleep quality and step count, we’re able to verify the integrity of the leaderboards and the XP earned through real physiological effort.

* **Holistic character “balancing”:** Most competing platforms have either a bias towards purely physical activity (ex- cardio in Strava or mental health betterment in Calm). However, we treat a user's RPG character in a way that any physical activity undergone by the user increases the “strength” attribute and good sleep reflects on higher “mana” for the avatar.

* **Narrative-driven retention:** Our application provides a reason for a user to build healthy consistent habits by branching off the “stories” which offsets the problem of the “3 week dropout” compared to applications like MyFitnessPal. The branching of stories tailored specifically for every user provides them with intrinsic motivation as opposed to just displaying dry graphs of their progress.

* **Accessibility and Inclusivity:** While most fitness platforms cater towards professionals, our platform’s gamified layer makes it more accessible and fun for non-athletes and gamers that find traditional habit building/fitness tracking platforms boring.

# **CHAPTER 02: STRATEGIC ANALYSIS** {#chapter-02:-strategic-analysis}

## **2.1 Porter’s five forces** {#2.1-porter’s-five-forces}

Porter's Five Forces framework is used to assess the structural attractiveness and competitive pressures of the digital wellness and health technology industry, which VitalQuest represents (Porter, 2008). VitalQuest competes in a fast expanding and innovative market by mixing wearable technology, AI-driven personalisation, and gamified engagement.

The structural pressures affecting VitalQuest are illustrated in Figure below.

**Threat of New Entrants – Moderate**

While mobile application development and cloud infrastructure are readily accessible, lowering technical entry barriers and recreating VitalQuest's entire ecosystem is more difficult. Wearable APIs, AI-based quest personalisation, encrypted health data management, and narrative-driven gamification all demand significant technology investment and multidisciplinary expertise to integrate. Furthermore, VitalQuest's guild-based community model generates early-stage network effects, which increase user retention while raising indirect entry barriers. Although startups can enter the market, maintaining a competitive advantage is considerably more difficult.

**Competitive Rivalry – High**

The digital wellness market is overrun with fitness monitoring apps, meditation platforms, and gamified productivity tools (Statista, 2023). Established competitors compete intensively over features, subscription price, and user engagement metrics. Furthermore, switching costs are low, intensifying competition. However, the majority of rivals operate in fragmented markets (fitness or mindfulness solely). VitalQuest minimises direct competition by presenting itself as a comprehensive RPG-based ecosystem that combines physical, nutritional, and psychological progress into a unified performance narrative. This integrated positioning enables partial differentiation in a crowded industry.

**Threat of Substitutes – Moderate**

Substitutes include gyms, personal trainers, therapeutic services, and free digital content like YouTube workouts. While there are many alternatives, VitalQuest's wearable automation and immersive narrative create a behavioural engagement loop which typical substitutes cannot readily imitate.

**Bargaining Power of Buyers – High**

Users are price sensitive and accustomed to freemium digital services (Kumar, 2014). Subscription weariness raises churn risk. VitalQuest reduces buyer power by leveraging strong engagement mechanisms such as AI-adaptive quests, level progression, and community rivalry, which enhances psychological switching costs.

**Bargaining Power of Suppliers – Moderate**

The dependency on wearable ecosystems and app distribution platforms adds supplier influence. However, multi-platform integration and competitive cloud service platforms mitigate dependency risk.

Overall, despite intense competition and buyer power, differentiation through integrated gamified automation elevates VitalQuest's competitive position.

## **2.2 SWOT analysis** {#2.2-swot-analysis}

The SWOT analysis assesses VitalQuest's internal capabilities and their alignment with external market conditions (Gürel and Tat, 2017).

**SWOT Analysis of VitalQuest**

|  | Positive | Negative |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Internal** | **Strengths** Bio-Sync automation Holistic integration Gamified retention | **Weaknesses** New entrant High dev cost Dependence on engagement |
| **External** | **Opportunities** Wearable growth Mental health awareness Corporate wellness | **Threats** Big tech competition Data privacy laws Rapid tech change |

**Strengths**

VitalQuest's main strength is its integrated "Bio-Sync" approach, which combines fitness, nutrition, and psychological well-being into a single gamified experience. Automated wearable data verification improves accuracy and trust by solving common issues with manual entry manipulation in gamified apps. The narrative-driven RPG progression system increases intrinsic motivation, while the freemium business model enables scalable user acquisition and recurring subscription income.

**Weaknesses**

As an upstart, VitalQuest lacks established brand recognition and a loyal customer base. AI personalisation engines, wearable integrations, and narrative systems demand significant initial investment and continuous technology improvements. Furthermore, the platform's success is heavily dependent on regular user engagement and community participation, making its early growth essential.

**Opportunities**

Global trends illustrate an increase in mental health awareness, wearable usage, and demand for personalised digital wellness solutions. Gamification's appeal among digitally native users indicates a high behavioural consistency with VitalQuest's concept. Corporate wellness collaboration and university partnership provide further growth opportunities.

**Threats**

Large tech companies may get into holistic health ecosystems. Rapid technological evolution may render features obsolete in the absence of continual innovation. Furthermore, severe data protection standards and potential cybersecurity risks create compliance and reputational problems. To reduce risks, VitalQuest can strategically utilise its technical integration strengths to capture wellness trends while focusing on brand trust, cybersecurity investment, and phased market development.

## **2.3 PESTEL analysis** {#2.3-pestel-analysis}

PESTEL study analyses the macro-environmental forces driving VitalQuest's strategic development (Johnson, Scholes and Whittington, 2020).

![][image2]

**Political Factors**

Government initiatives fostering digital health transformation and preventive healthcare create an favourable environment for wellness platforms. However, regulatory compliance requirements for cross-border data transfer and digital health services make operations more complex.

**Economic Factors**

Consumer spending on digital subscriptions may vary amid economic downturns. However, the global trend toward remote work and digital lifestyles is driving demand for online wellness solutions. VitalQuest's freemium framework lowers entrance costs, boosting resilience during financial crises.

**Social Factors**

Rising awareness of mental health issues, sedentary lifestyles, and work-related stress drives demand for integrated well-being solutions. Social gamification consistent with digital-native behaviour patterns, notably among Generation Z and Millennials (Hamari et al., 2014). Community-based motivator systems reflect changing social engagement preferences.

**Technological Factors**

Advancements in artificial intelligence, wearable sensors, and cloud computing promptly enhance VitalQuest's scalability and personalisation possibilities. To remain competitive in the midst of rapid technological innovation, constant system upgrades and cybersecurity reinforcement are required.

**Environmental Factors**

Digital wellness solutions eliminate the need for physical commuting to gyms, indirectly promoting sustainable lifestyle trends. Eco-conscious branding and corporate ESG alignment can improve collaborations and public perception.

**Legal Factors**

Strict data protection frameworks, such as GDPR, and evolving digital health standards necessitate secure data encryption, consent management systems, and transparent privacy policies (European Commission, 2018). Compliance is crucial for preserving user trust and avoiding reputational damage. Among these considerations, advances in technology and legal compliance represent the most significant strategic impact on VitalQuest's long-term sustainability.

## **2.4 Blue ocean strategy** {#2.4-blue-ocean-strategy}

The Blue Ocean Strategy is used to highlight how VitalQuest develops uncontested market space rather than competing primarily in the saturated fitness or meditation sectors (Kim and Mauborgne, 2015).

**Blue Ocean Strategy Analysis of VitalQuest**

|                            Eliminate Fragmented single-purpose wellness apps Spreadsheet-style calorie tracking Isolated mental or physical health scoring Passive, static content consumption |                                Reduce Manual data entry burden User friction in tracking routines Overemphasis on performance-only metrics Feature overload without personalization |
| :---- | :---- |
|                             **Raise** User engagement through narrative immersion AI-driven personalization of quests Community accountability via guild systems Behavioural motivation through gamified rewards |                                 **Create** Bio-Sync automated health verification engine Integrated holistic health performance score RPG-based life progression ecosystem Cross-dimensional wellness storyline (physical \+ mental \+ nutrition) |

Through the ERRC framework:

**Eliminate** **–** Fragmented single-purpose health apps and manual self-reporting systems.

**Reduce –** Data complexity and disengagement from spreadsheet-style tracking.

**Raise –** User interaction, personalisation, and community-based motivation.

**Create –** An immersive RPG-based "Bio-Sync" ecosystem that combines physical, mental, and nutritional progress into one performance plot.

Traditional wellness platforms compete through incremental feature enhancements and subscription pricing. VitalQuest transforms ordinary health practices into a narrative-driven progression journey, shifting competitiveness towards experience engagement and behavioural improvement. This repositioning decreases direct price rivalry while increasing perceived user value.

## **2.5 Strategic Summary** {#2.5-strategic-summary}

The digital wellness market has been driven by severe competition along with significant buyer purchasing power. VitalQuest thus employs a differentiation strategy, emphasising on complete integration, AI-driven personalisation, and immersive gamification rather than competing on cost (Porter, 2008).

Its primary competitive advantage is derived from merging wearable-verified data with RPG narrative progression to create behavioural engagement and reduce churn. The platform's long-term scalability is dependent on improving community network effects, extending wearable integrations, and sustaining technology innovation.

Strategic priorities include continuous AI development, strong cybersecurity compliance, and incremental brand expansion to establish credibility. VitalQuest positions itself as a defensible and scalable digital wellness ecosystem in a highly competitive technological world by utilising Bio-Sync automation and integrated well-being tracking.

# **CHAPTER 03: DESIGN SPECIFICATION** {#chapter-03:-design-specification}

## **3.1 Functional requirements** {#3.1-functional-requirements}

1. **User Registration and Profile Setup (FR1):** Users must create accounts via email, social login (Google/Facebook), or biometrics, inputting baseline health data (age, weight, goals) to generate a customized avatar. This enables personalized quests, differentiating from manual-entry apps like Habitica.

2. **Bio-Sync Integration (FR2):** Seamless connection to wearables (Apple Health, Google Fit) for automatic data import (steps, heart rate, sleep). The system verifies and converts data into in-game XP, rewards, and avatar stats, ensuring authenticity and reducing user effort unique to VitalQuest for preventing "cheating" common in gamified apps.

3. **Quest and Challenge System (FR3):** Generate daily/weekly quests blending physical (e.g., "Run 5km to defeat the Forest Guardian"), nutritional (e.g., "Log balanced meals to restore health"), and psychological (e.g., "Meditate 10min to recharge mana"). Quests adapt via AI based on user progress and preferences, fostering long-term engagement.

4. **Narrative Progression (FR4):** An evolving storyline where user actions influence plot branches (e.g., consistent mindfulness unlocks "Wisdom Paths"). This includes audio narratives and visual storyboards, providing intrinsic motivation beyond basic tracking in apps like MyFitnessPal.

5. **Social and Community Features (FR5):** Users join "guilds" for group challenges, share achievements on leaderboards, and compete in events. This builds network effects, encouraging viral growth and retention through social accountability.

6. **Progress Tracking and Analytics (FR6):** Dashboard displaying avatar stats, progress graphs, and predictive insights (e.g., "Continue this streak to reach Level 10 in 2 weeks"). Includes exportable reports for professional use, adding value for serious users.

7. **Monetization Integration (FR7):** In-app store for premium items (e.g., custom avatar skins, advanced quests) and subscription tiers unlocking ad-free experience and expert coaching modules.

8. **Feedback and Support (FR8):** In-app chat for user queries, AI-driven tips, and community forums to report issues or suggest quests, ensuring continuous improvement.

## **3.2 Non-functional requirements** {#3.2-non-functional-requirements}

1. **Performance (NFR1):** System must load pages in \<2 seconds and handle 10,000 concurrent users with 99.9% uptime, supporting scalability for global expansion.

2. **Security (NFR2):** GDPR-compliant data encryption, secure API integrations for wearables, and two-factor authentication to protect sensitive health data, building trust in a privacy-sensitive market.

3. **Usability (NFR3):** Intuitive mobile-first interface with accessibility features for diverse users, including beginners and those with disabilities, to maximize adoption.

4. **Reliability (NFR4):** Automated backups and error handling to ensure data sync accuracy \>98%, preventing loss of progress that could lead to user churn.

5. **Scalability (NFR5):** Cloud-based architecture to auto-scale with user growth, accommodating seasonal spikes.

6. **Maintainability (NFR6):** Modular code design for easy updates, such as adding new wearable integrations, to keep the platform innovative against competitors.

## **3.3 Use case diagram** {#3.3-use-case-diagram}

![][image3]

## **3.4 Use case descriptions** {#3.4-use-case-descriptions}

| UC ID | 1 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name** | Sync Wearable Data (Bio-Sync) |
| **Participating Actors** | User (primary), Wearable Platform (external system) |
| **Entry Condition** | The user is logged in. The user has a completed profile OR is prompted to complete required profile fields. |
| **Flow of Events** | The user opens **Bio-Sync / Connections**. The system shows available wearable integrations (e.g., Apple Health / Google Fit / Fitbit). User selects a wearable platform and grants permissions (OAuth / device permissions). The system pulls biometric/activity data (steps, heart rate, sleep) for the allowed time range. Bio-Sync engine validates incoming data (timestamps, duplicates, anomaly checks). The system converts verified data into **XP, rewards, and avatar stats**. System updates quest progress and dashboard indicators. System confirms sync success (or shows error \+ retry steps). |
| **Exit Condition** | Latest wearable data is stored; avatar stats and quest progress reflect the synced data. |
| **Special Requirements** |  Security \+ privacy: encrypted transport/storage; secure API integrations (NFR2). Reliability: sync accuracy target \>98%, retries and error handling (NFR4). Maintainability: modular integration layer to add new wearables easily (NFR6).  |

| UC ID | 2 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name** | Participate in Quests |
| **Participating Actors** | User (primary), Wearable Platform (supporting, for verification) |
| **Entry Condition** | The user is logged in. Users have active quests (daily/weekly) available. |
| **Flow of Events** | The system generates quests (physical, nutrition, psychological) for the day/week. The user views the quest list and selects a quest to start. The system shows quest narrative context \+ success criteria (e.g., minutes, steps, logged meals). The user performs the real-world activity. System verifies completion via Bio-Sync data **(includes UC2 verification dependency)**. If verified, the system awards XP, items, and updates avatar stats (including “mana” balance rules). System unlocks/updates storyline branches based on the completed quest. System shows results screen: rewards, streaks, next recommended quest.  |
| **Exit Condition** | Quest is marked completed/failed/partial; rewards and storyline state are updated accordingly.  |
| **Special Requirements** | Performance: quest screens and reward updates must feel instant/near-real-time (NFR1). Anti-cheat: automated verification \+ anomaly detection to reduce fake progress (ties to FR2). Usability/accessibility: clear objectives, readable UI, optional voice guidance (NFR3).  |

| UC ID | 3 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name** | Engage in Social Features |
| **Participating Actors** | User (primary), System Admin (supporting), Other Users (secondary users) |
| **Entry Condition** | The user is logged in. Social/community features are enabled for the user account (and the user accepts community rules).  |
| **Flow of Events** | The user opens **Community/Guilds**. The user joins an existing guild or creates a new guild. Users participate in guild challenges/events (group quests, seasonal competitions). The system updates leaderboards and displays achievements. Users can share achievements and react/comment within community spaces. Users report abuse/content issues if needed. Admin reviews reports and moderates (warn/remove content, suspend accounts, etc.). |
| **Exit Condition** | Guild membership and community actions (posts, scores, leaderboard positions) are saved and visible to relevant users. |
| **Special Requirements** |  Scalability: handle spikes (events, New Year resolution season), scalable community services (NFR5). Moderation: admin tooling, reporting workflow, audit logs (supports safety \+ trust). Privacy controls: user can limit what stats are shared publicly (aligns with NFR2 spirit).  |

| UC ID  | 4 |
| :---- | :---- |
| **Use Case Name** | View Progress and Analytics |
| **Participating Actors** | User (primary) |
| **Entry Condition** | The user is logged in. The system has stored progress data (from quests and/or wearable sync). |
| **Flow of Events** | The user opens **Dashboard/Analytics**. System loads avatar stats, streaks, and progress graphs **(uses latest Bio-Sync data; depends on UC1)**. The system shows insights/predictions (e.g., projected level-up date at current pace). User filters by timeframe or category (fitness/nutrition/mindfulness). User exports a report for personal/professional use (PDF/CSV export). |
| **Exit Condition** | Analytics are displayed and (if chosen) an export file/report is generated for the user.  |
| **Special Requirements** |  Usability: easy-to-read graphs, accessible design (NFR3). Reliability: data consistency (sync \+ quest completions reflect correctly) (NFR4). Performance: dashboards load quickly even with large history (NFR1).  |

## **3.5 Activity Diagrams** {#3.5-activity-diagrams}

![][image4]  
A sequence diagram was selected because it effectively illustrates the dynamic flow of interactions between the user and the main system components over time. In this gamified digital wellbeing fitness application, setting and tracking fitness goals involves multiple connected processes, including goal creation, reward assignment, activity logging, progress tracking, and achievement updates. Since these actions occur in a specific sequence and require coordination between several components, the sequence diagram provides a clear representation of how the system operates step by step. Therefore, this modelling technique is appropriate because it captures the system’s behaviour logically and shows how the application delivers real-time feedback and gamified user engagement.

## **3.6 UML Diagram** {#3.6-uml-diagram}

![][image5]

# **CHAPTER 04: INDIVIDUAL ROLES IN BUSINESS** {#chapter-04:-individual-roles-in-business}

## **1\. Himash Bandara | 20210756 | w1953862** {#1.-himash-bandara-|-20210756-|-w1953862}

If the VitalQuest business idea were implemented as a real company, my role would be the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) and Business & Partnerships Lead. In this position, I would be responsible for shaping the overall business vision, defining the mission of the company, identifying target markets, and developing sustainable revenue models. I would also focus on building strategic partnerships with fitness brands, wearable technology companies, and corporate wellness providers in order to expand the platform’s reach and create new business opportunities.

I believe I am suitable for this role because of my interest in entrepreneurship, technology-driven business ideas, and digital product development. My academic background in computing, combined with my understanding of digital platforms and emerging technologies, allows me to recognize innovative opportunities in the health and wellness market. Additionally, my experience working on technology-related projects has helped me develop skills in problem solving, strategic thinking, and communication, which are essential qualities for leading a startup.

The role of CEO is critical for achieving the strategic goals of VitalQuest. The platform aims to build a strong user base and generate revenue through premium subscriptions, in-app purchases, and corporate wellness partnerships. As CEO, I would ensure that the company’s strategy remains focused on delivering value to users while maintaining a scalable and profitable business model. By guiding the overall direction of the company and forming partnerships with relevant industry stakeholders, the business can expand its market presence and achieve long-term growth.

## **2\. Ametha Jayawardena | 20220885 | w1954086** {#2.-ametha-jayawardena-|-20220885-|-w1954086}

If VitalQuest was to come to life as a business, my position in the company would translate to Chief Marketing Officer (CMO). I would be responsible for researching and analysing the competition in the domain and determining the position of the company, the competitive strategy and the overall direction of our brand. Additionally, there would also be a need to identify trends in the targeted customer markets and features which competitive companies on the domain implements. I think I’m suitable for this role since I have experience in the field of marketing, especially in academics related to it as well as I have followed modules such as Digital Marketing in university. Additionally, I was responsible for conducting the competitor analysis and identifying gaps in the current market when it comes to the current coursework. Alongside that the problem statement was thoroughly identified, phrased and analysed by me to provide an optimal marketing solution. Also, conducting research on which business model and how this business would be operating in the frame of e-business directly increases my suitability for the role of CMO as the marketing strategy is dependent on it. I have worked closely with the other team members to identify who our business idea will be targeting as customers and analyzed if it is feasible to ensure that our identified target market would show interest in the application especially by looking at competitors and also by looking at competitors I was able to identify what would make our idea truly unique.

**Mentoring reflection**

The group mentoring sessions helped me and my team to carry out our successful coursework first and foremost by helping us refine our initial business idea and the lecturers helping us refine the problem. The meetings I’ve had among my teammates helped me get much essential feedback on which business model we will be following for VitalQuest and for me to give feedback on what kind of requirements the platform we aim to build should have. A mention needs to be made that the business model categorization was conducted in a joint effort with Jaufer Ahamed Zameeh.

## **3\. Jayali Ranasinghe | 20200817 | w1837940** {#3.-jayali-ranasinghe-|-20200817-|-w1837940}

If VitalQuest were to become a real business, I would serve as Chief Operating Officer (COO) and Strategy & Growth Lead. My responsibilities would include analysing the competitive environment, identifying strategic possibilities, and guiding the platform's long-term growth direction. This includes assessing industry conditions, analysing market trends, and sustaining VitalQuest's strong strategic position within the digital wellness ecosystem.

This role corresponds to my contribution to the coursework, in which I completed a strategic analysis using Porter's Five Forces, SWOT, PESTEL, and Blue Ocean Strategy. Using these techniques, I analysed industry competition, internal capabilities, macro-environmental impacts, and value-innovation possibilities. My work demonstrated how VitalQuest can differentiate itself through AI-driven personalisation, wearable integration, and immersive gamification.

I believe I am qualified for this position because I am able to connect business objectives with analytical thinking. I can assess strategic risks, discover market opportunities, and present growth-oriented recommendations to facilitate the company's long-term scalability. This role is strategically significant since VitalQuest operates in a highly competitive digital wellness industry where long-term success requires strong strategic positioning, innovation, and sustained differentiation.

**Mentoring reflection**

During the group mentoring sessions, we received helpful advice on improving our business concept and enhancing the analytical depth of our work. The feedback supported us to focus on how our business idea differs from other platforms, as well as ensure that our strategic tools clearly supported the concept's uniqueness. This guidance assisted me in refining the strategic analysis by connecting each tool to VitalQuest's competitive advantage.

## **4\. Suseenthiran Arulraj Rumeasiyan | 20221035 | w1954104** {#4.-suseenthiran-arulraj-rumeasiyan-|-20221035-|-w1954104}

If VitalQuest moves forward, I would take the role of Product Manager. My responsibilities would be translating the business vision into an implementable product by defining user requirements, functional and non-functional requirements, and use cases, ensuring that features such as wearable sync, gamified quests, and analytics remain usable, secure, and aligned to revenue goals. This fits my contribution to the coursework where I produced the rich picture, user requirements, FR/NFR, and the use case diagram.

I am suitable for this role because I can bridge business and technology: I can turn user needs into clear specifications, maintain requirement traceability, and design flows that reduce user friction. This role is strategically important because VitalQuest’s differentiation depends on a seamless experience and trustworthy verification, strong requirements and UX directly support retention, subscription conversion, and user trust.

Mentoring reflection: During our mentoring sessions, we received feedback to our ideas and how we can analyze them. These sessions helped me refine the use case model and align it with business uniqueness.

## **5\. Jaufer Ahamed Zameeh | 20220100 | w1953590** {#5.-jaufer-ahamed-zameeh-|-20220100-|-w1953590}

If VitalQuest were to become a real business, I would serve as the Chief Technology Officer (CTO) and Technical Architecture Lead. My responsibilities would include designing the overall system architecture, ensuring the platform is technically feasible, scalable, secure, and aligned with the business model. This would involve overseeing the application’s technical infrastructure, modelling system interactions, defining how core modules such as  gamified quests, analytics, and social/community features integrate, and ensuring that the digital platform can support future growth and innovation.

This role directly reflects my contribution to the coursework, where I was responsible for the formal use case description, UML modelling, and e-business categorisation of the business. Through this work, I translated the business concept into a structured technical model by showing how users, external systems, and platform processes interact within the application. I also explained the business from an e-commerce perspective by identifying it as a dotcom, pure-play, B2C digital platform with future B2B opportunities, which helped connect the technical system design to the platform’s long-term commercial viability.

I believe I am suitable for this role because I am able to combine technical thinking with business understanding. I can break down complex system behaviour into clear models, design scalable platform structures, and ensure that technical solutions support the company’s strategic goals. This role is highly important because the success of VitalQuest depends not only on having a strong business concept, but also on having a reliable and scalable digital architecture that can support user growth, wearable integrations, real-time analytics, secure transactions, and long-term platform expansion.

**Mentoring reflection**

During the group mentoring sessions, we received helpful guidance on strengthening the link between our business idea and the technical analysis. This feedback helped me refine the use case and UML diagrams so they reflected the platform’s uniqueness rather than seeming too generic. It also helped me better understand how the technical architecture supports the business model, user experience, and scalability. As a result, I improved my section by aligning the system design more closely with the strategic purpose of the business. A note should be made that the business model categorization was completed collaboratively with Ametha Jayawardena.

# 

# **APPENDIX A \- VIDEO PITCH TOWARDS INVESTORS** {#appendix-a---video-pitch-towards-investors}

The below link leads to a video pitch created towards potential investors as part of an answer to the last section of the coursework specification’s first question.

[https://youtu.be/nU7q0GLPTnE](https://youtu.be/nU7q0GLPTnE) 

# **APPENDIX B – DETAILED PORTER’S FIVE FORCES ANALYSIS** {#appendix-b-–-detailed-porter’s-five-forces-analysis}

| Force | Key Industry Drivers | Impact on VitalQuest | Strategic Response |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Threat of New Entrants – Moderate** | Low basic app development challenges. cloud infrastructure availability; increasing need for digital wellbeing; nonetheless, considerable integration complexity (AI, wearables, cybersecurity). | While entering into fitness apps is easy, replicating VitalQuest's AI-powered Bio-Sync engine, narrative gamification, and secure wearable verification needs significant investment and technical skill. | Invest in continual innovation, improve AI personalisation, establish credibility for the brand early on, and create strong community network effects to raise indirect entry barriers. |
| **Competitive Rivalry – High** | The market is saturated with fitness, meditation, and productivity apps; switching costs are minimal; subscription competition is high; and updates to features are frequent. | High competition imposes more pressure on user acquisition and retention. Users can easily switch to alternate platforms. | Instead of competing on price, differentiate by complete integration (physical, mental, and nutritional), immersive RPG storyline, and community-based engagement. |
| **Threat of Substitutes – Moderate** | Offline gyms, personal trainers, therapeutic services, YouTube videos, and standalone wellness apps. | Substitutes offer alternative health options, particularly free content platforms. | Increase behavioural engagement with automated wearable verification and gamified progress systems that traditional alternatives cannot replicate. |
| **Bargaining Power of Buyers – High** | Subscription fatigue, price sensitivity, freemium expectations, and a large number of alternatives. | Strong buyer power increases churn risk and limits pricing flexibility. | Use the freemium entry model to boost switching costs through AI-adaptive tasks, social guild participation, and long-term advancement rewards. |
| **Bargaining Power of Suppliers – Moderate**  | Dependence on wearable APIs (Apple Health, Fitbit), app store distribution control, and cloud infrastructure providers. | Platform dependency may have an impact on data access, pricing, and compliance obligations. | Diversify wearable integrations, preserve multi-cloud scalability options, and assure regulatory compliance to reduce supplier leverage. |

# **APPENDIX C – EXTENDED SWOT STRATEGIC MAPPING** {#appendix-c-–-extended-swot-strategic-mapping}

1. **Strengths–Opportunities (SO Strategies)**

Using strengths to exploit opportunities.

| Strength	 | Opportunity	 | Strategic Action |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Bio-Sync automated wearable integration	 | Growing wearable adoption  | Position VitalQuest as a reliable automated wellbeing verification platform to attract wearable-driven users. |
| Gamified RPG progression system	 | Gen Z gamification acceptance | Engage university students and young professionals with immersive, story-driven health challenges. |
| Holistic integration (physical \+ mental \+ nutrition)	 | Rising mental health awareness | Promote VitalQuest as a whole wellness service rather than just fitness tracking. |
| Freemium revenue model	 | Corporate wellness growth | Provide scalable enterprise wellness packages with premium features to organisations. |

2. **Weaknesses–Opportunities (WO Strategies)**

Using opportunities to overcome weaknesses.

| Weakness	 | Opportunity	 | Strategic Action |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Lack of brand recognition	 | Growing digital wellness demand | Use influencer marketing and university collaborations to increase brand visibility. |
| High development costs	 | Corporate wellness investment | Secure B2B partnerships to earn early revenue and reduce development costs. |
| New market entrant	 | Expanding wearable ecosystem | Integrate with several wearable brands to increase credibility and ecosystem trust. |

3. **Strengths–Threats (ST Strategies)**

Using strengths to mitigate threats.

| Strength	 | Threat	 | Strategic Action |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| AI-driven personalization	 | Big tech competition | Instead of competing on scale, differentiate with a distinct holistic RPG positioning. |
| Secure Bio-Sync verification  | Data privacy regulations | Use strong encryption and compliance messaging to get a competitive advantage. |
| Community guild model	 | High buyer switching power | Long-term progression systems could assist increase emotional switching costs. |

4. **Weaknesses–Threats (WT Strategies)**

Minimizing weaknesses and avoiding threats.

| Weakness	 | Threat	 | Strategic Action |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Dependence on wearable APIs	 | Platform policy changes  | Diversify integrations to lessen dependency on a single ecosystem. |
| New brand credibility	 | Cybersecurity risks | Invest early on compliance certifications and transparent data policy. |
| Engagement dependency	 | Rapid tech change | Maintain a constant feature innovation roadmap. |

# **APPENDIX D – EXPANDED PESTEL ENVIRONMENTAL ANALYSIS** {#appendix-d-–-expanded-pestel-environmental-analysis}

| Factor	 | Key External Conditions	 | Strategic Implications for VitalQuest |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Political** | Government assistance with digital health transformation, preventive healthcare efforts, and cross-border health data governance policies. | Political support for digital wellness promotes the long-term need for preventive platforms such as VitalQuest. However, cross-border requirements necessitate robust data localisation techniques and compliance management systems. |
| **Economic** | Subscription sensitivity during economic downturns, increased corporate wellness investing and rising healthcare expenses all encourage preventive solutions. | Economic instability may have an impact on individual subscriber acceptance, emphasising the significance of a freemium approach. Corporate wellness partnerships offer reliable revenue diversification and scalability. |
| **Social** | Rising mental health awareness, sedentary lifestyle trends, Gen Z inclination for gamified digital experiences, and post-pandemic home-based wellness uptake. | Social trends substantially support VitalQuest's holistic and gamified methodology. Positioning the platform as a tool for healthy life advancement increases its relevance among digital natives. |
| **Technological** | Rapid AI development, wearable ecosystem expansion, cloud-based SaaS scalability, and rising cybersecurity requirements. | Technological innovation represents both an opportunity and a risk. Continuous AI enhancement and wearable integration expansion are important for maintaining a competitive advantage. Investing in cybersecurity and system scalability promotes trust and long-term growth. |
| **Environmental** | Increased sustainability awareness, reduced commuting through digital services and ESG-driven corporate wellness initiatives. | Digital wellness complements sustainability narratives, facilitating ESG cooperation and eco-conscious branding initiatives. |
| **Legal** | GDPR and international data protection legislation, health data classification criteria, AI transparency restrictions and platform compliance guidelines. | Legal compliance is strategically important. To mitigate reputational and regulatory concerns, VitalQuest must establish encrypted data storage, transparent consent management, and ethical AI governance. |

# **APPENDIX E – EXTENDED BLUE OCEAN STRATEGIC ANALYSIS** {#appendix-e-–-extended-blue-ocean-strategic-analysis}

1. **Justification for Selecting Blue Ocean Strategy**

Blue Ocean Strategy was chosen to complement Porter's Five Forces model by shifting the focus away from competitive pressure within the existing digital wellness market to value innovation and market space creation. While Porter's methodology assesses rivalry intensity, Blue Ocean Strategy analyses how VitalQuest might redefine consumer value while reducing direct competition. Given that VitalQuest incorporates physical fitness, mental well-being, and nutrition into an AI-driven RPG ecosystem, this model is ideal for demonstrating how the platform reconstructs industry boundaries rather than competing purely on incremental feature enhancements.

2. **Industry Value Factors in the Current Digital Wellness Market**

Traditional fitness and wellness apps usually compete on,

* Calorie tracking accuracy  
* Workout library size  
* Meditation session quantity  
* Performance measurements and analytics.  
* Subscription Pricing Models

These value considerations lead to feature-based rivalry and pricing competition. Most platforms operate in segregated verticals (fitness only or meditation only), resulting in fragmentation and low overall participation. This competitive structure provides a "red ocean" environment defined by intense competition and low switching costs.

3. **ERRC-Based Strategic Repositioning**

As illustrated in the ERRC grid in section 2.5 VitalQuest strategically,

* Eliminates fragmented, single-purpose wellness tracking.  
* Reduces manual data entry and metric overload.  
* Raises behavioural engagement through narrative immersion and AI-driven objectives.  
* Creates the unified Bio-Sync automated verification engine and integrated life-progression ecosystem.

Rather than competing on content volume or subscription pricing, VitalQuest remakes the user experience as an immersive, multidimensional  well-being progression.

4. **Value Innovation and Competitive Shift**

VitalQuest changes competitiveness from functional tracking to experience transformation.

Traditional platforms focus on, 

* Isolated wellness information.  
* Static progress dashboards  
* Individualised tracking without narrative context.

In contrast, VitalQuest emphasises,

* AI-Personalized Quest Evolution  
* Community guild accountability  
* Holistic health scoring  
* Identity development through narratives

This repositioning raises perceived value while lowering direct price comparisons to competitors. Emotional engagement and progression-based incentive reduce switching costs and improve long-term retention.

5. **Sustainability and Risk of Imitation**

Although Blue Ocean positioning lowers immediate competition overlap, successful value creation may prompt imitation by established businesses. To maintain its distinct position, VitalQuest must consistently invest in,

* Advanced AI personalisation algorithms.  
* Extension of wearable integrations  
* Enhancing the impact of the community network  
* Narrative Content Innovation

By integrating technological complexity with behavioural engagement systems, VitalQuest creates layered differentiation that is more difficult to imitate than simple feature additions.

# **ETHICAL AND RESPONSIBLE USE OF GENERATIVE AI TOOLS** {#ethical-and-responsible-use-of-generative-ai-tools}

The team made use of Generative AI tools including ChatGPT and Gemini in the initial stages to assess and evaluate the feasibility of the business idea and to brainstorm features the application needs as an MVP. Moreover, it was used in getting guidance on  fetching information such as definitions and types of business models, digital strategies and examples on how UML diagrams can be drawn.

However, we confirm that we have not used AI to generate content that we are presenting as our own work, and we remain fully responsible for the ideas, analysis, and conclusions in this submission

Ametha Jayawardena \-

# **REFERENCES** {#references}

* European Commission (2018) General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). Available at: [https://commission.europa.eu/publications/publications-general-data-protection-regulation-gdpr\_en](https://commission.europa.eu/publications/publications-general-data-protection-regulation-gdpr_en) (Accessed: 2 March 2026).  
* Gürel, E. and Tat, M. (2017) ‘SWOT analysis: A theoretical review’, Journal of International Social Research, 10(51), pp. 994–1006.  
* Hamari, J., Koivisto, J. and Sarsa, H. (2014) ‘Does gamification work? A literature review of empirical studies on gamification’, 47th Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences, pp. 3025–3034.  
* Johnson, G., Scholes, K. and Whittington, R. (2020) Exploring Strategy. 12th edn. Harlow: Pearson.  
* Kim, W.C. and Mauborgne, R. (2015) Blue Ocean Strategy: How to Create Uncontested Market Space and Make Competition Irrelevant. Boston: Harvard Business Review Press.  
* Kumar, V. (2014) ‘Making “freemium” work’, Harvard Business Review, 92(5), pp. 27–29.  
* Porter, M.E. (2008) ‘The Five Competitive Forces That Shape Strategy’, Harvard Business Review, 86(1), pp. 78–93.  
* Statista (2023) Digital health market size worldwide. Available at:[https://www.statista.com/forecasts/387867/value-of-worldwide-digital-health-market-forecast-by-segment/](https://www.statista.com/forecasts/387867/value-of-worldwide-digital-health-market-forecast-by-segment/)  (Accessed: 2 March 2026).  
* Yadav, K.B. (n.d) E-Commerce Business Models Explained. Available at [E-Commerce Business Models Explained | PDF | E Commerce | Service Industries](https://www.scribd.com/document/707448619/Unit-2-e-com-or-e-bus-model)  
* Freemium: Definition, Best Practices, Benefits and Examples (n.d). Available at [Freemium: Definition, Best Practices, Benefits and Examples \- Zuora](https://www.zuora.com/glossary/freemium-business-model/)  
* Scope of e-Business : B2B | B2C | C2C | Intra B-Commerce (2025). Available at [Scope of e-Business : B2B | B2C | C2C | Intra B-Commerce \- GeeksforGeeks](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/business-studies/scope-of-e-business-b2b-commerce-b2c-commercec2c-commerceintra-b-commerce/)
